paper_title: Isotopic Analysis and Environmental Monitoring in the Assynt Area

methods.description: Collection of monthly bulk precipitation samples for isotopic analysis to understand environmental conditions.

steps.description: Monthly bulk precipitation samples for isotopic analysis were collected from Inchnadamph (3 km from the cave), using a plastic rain gauge, 0.5 m above ground level; paraffin oil was added to prevent evaporation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect monthly precipitation samples using a rain gauge with paraffin oil

steps.description: Each sample was taken on the last day of the month from November 2003-November 2005, but there were two missing months in the second year of sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: schedule sample collection on the last day of each month

methods.description: Collection of cave drip water samples from diverse sites to analyze hydrological conditions and isotopic composition.

steps.description: Cave drip water samples were collected from thirteen, hydrologically diverse drip sites over two hydrological years, at a resolution of two to three months.
steps.searchable_summary: collect drip water samples from multiple sites every 2-3 months

steps.description: Drip waters were collected in high density polypropylene (HDPE) bottles over a period of 24-28 h; after collection they were sealed and refrigerated until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store collected water samples in HDPE bottles and refrigerate

steps.description: Ancillary information collected includes the drip interval (time between drips) and electroconductivity (EC), the latter using a WTW Multi 340i instrument, automatically correcting data to 25°C (±0.5%).
steps.searchable_summary: measure drip interval and electroconductivity using WTW Multi 340i

methods.description: Measurement of oxygen and hydrogen isotopes in water samples to determine isotopic composition.

steps.description: Oxygen and hydrogen isotope measurements of waters were carried out at the University of East Anglia using CO2-equilibration for δ18O (precision 0.06‰) and by reduction using glassy carbon chips in continuous flow for δD measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotope measurements using CO2-equilibration and glassy carbon chips

steps.description: Each groundwater sample was replicated and an internal laboratory reference water was measured after every third sample.
steps.searchable_summary: replicate samples and measure reference water for validation

methods.description: Measurement of isotopic ratios in contemporary calcite samples to understand past environmental conditions.

steps.description: The isotopic ratio of the contemporary calcite was measured at the University of Innsbruck, Austria.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotopic ratio of calcite samples

steps.description: Calcite was sampled at high resolution from two Tartair stalagmites SU96-7 and SU03-2, using a micromill system, using continuous trenches, with a resolution of 0.1 mm in the growth direction.
steps.searchable_summary: sample calcite using micromill system at 0.1 mm resolution

steps.description: The drilled stalagmite powders were then analyzed for δ18O using a continuous-flow isotope-ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ18O in calcite powders using isotope-ratio mass spectrometer

methods.description: Correlation of historical precipitation and temperature data to understand environmental trends and calculate soil moisture budget.

steps.description: Precipitation and temperature data for Stornoway from AD 1873 to 2006 were obtained from the UK meteorological office.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch historical precipitation and temperature data from UK meteorological office

steps.description: This was correlated with existing precipitation and temperature data for Knockanrock, 80 km SE of Stornaway and 12 km SW of the Tartair site.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate Stornoway data with Knockanrock data

steps.description: A positive linear relationship was found between Stornoway and Knockanrock for both temperature and precipitation, both significant at the 99.9% confidence interval.
steps.searchable_summary: model linear relationship between Stornoway and Knockanrock data

steps.description: This relationship was then used to calculate Knockanrock precipitation and temperature from July 1874 to the present.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate historical Knockanrock data using linear relationship

steps.description: The soil moisture budget was calculated from these data using the equation of Thornthwaite (1948).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate soil moisture budget using Thornthwaite equation
